responsibilities: Architect and implement RTL designs in Verilog/SystemVerilog for complex digital blocks within SoC and ASIC chip projects.
Define microarchitecture specifications translating system-level requirements into synthesizable, timing-closure-ready RTL implementations.
Perform clock domain crossing (CDC) analysis and resolve metastability issues across asynchronous interfaces in multi-clock designs.
Drive logic synthesis using Cadence Genus or Synopsys Design Compiler to achieve power, performance, and area (PPA) targets.
Collaborate with verification engineers to define test plans, coverage goals, and debug RTL functional failures.
Integrate IP blocks using AMBA bus protocols such as AXI, AHB, and APB into larger SoC architectures.
Apply low-power design techniques including clock gating, power gating, and multi-voltage domains using UPF methodology.
Work with physical design and DFT teams to ensure RTL is synthesis-friendly and scan-insertion compatible.
Conduct design reviews and lint checks to maintain RTL coding standards and ensure design reusability.
Support tapeout milestones by resolving timing violations, synthesis warnings, and sign-off checklist items.
